description: Dispatch v1 - Project-local CLI and embedded broker for coordinating long-lived agent sessions and lightweight scripts inside a single cell

userStories.title: Project scaffold and CLI skeleton
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Rust project initialized with all dependencies, clap derive CLI with subcommand stubs, lib/bin split, error types, and tracing setup.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo new with edition 2021 and all dependencies in Cargo.toml (clap, serde, toml, thiserror, tracing, tracing-subscriber, tokio, reqwest, semver, uuid)
Clap derive CLI with subcommands: serve, register, team, send, listen, heartbeat
lib.rs exposes modules, main.rs is thin wrapper that parses args and delegates
Error enum with thiserror in errors module
Tracing subscriber setup with rolling file appender and DISPATCH_LOG env var support
arg_required_else_help = true, version, about, after_help with documented exit codes
profile.release with strip = true and lto = true
cargo build succeeds with no warnings
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Config resolution and cell identity
userStories.description: As a user, I want Dispatch to resolve project-local config by searching upward for dispatch.config.toml and derive a stable cell identity from the project root path.
acceptanceCriteria: Config struct with serde + toml and deny_unknown_fields
Search current directory and parent directories for dispatch.config.toml
Config supports at least cell_id and backend fields
Derive stable fallback cell ID by hashing the project root path when not configured
CLI flag --cell-id and DISPATCH_CELL_ID env var override config value
Layer precedence: CLI flag > env var > config file > derived fallback
Tests for config resolution, fallback identity derivation, and override precedence
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Embedded broker server with dispatch serve
userStories.description: As a user, I want dispatch serve to start an embedded local broker on a Unix domain socket with in-memory state and no external dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: dispatch serve starts async tokio server listening on a Unix domain socket
Socket path derived from cell identity in a project-local or temp location
JSON-line request/response protocol over the socket
In-memory state struct for worker registrations and message queues
Detect and prevent duplicate broker for same cell with clear error message
Restarting the broker clears all runtime state from the previous run
Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM cleans up socket file
Tests for server startup and duplicate cell detection
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Client transport module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a client module that connects to the broker Unix socket and sends/receives JSON requests for use by all CLI subcommands.
acceptanceCriteria: Client struct that resolves and connects to broker Unix socket by cell identity
Async send_request and receive_response methods with JSON serialization
Clear error message when broker is not running or socket not found
Request and response types defined as shared enums used by both client and server
All CLI subcommands (register, team, send, listen, heartbeat) wired to use client
Tests for client connection and broker-not-running error
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Worker registration command
userStories.description: As a worker, I want to register with dispatch register providing my name, role, description, and optional capabilities, and receive a worker ID back.
acceptanceCriteria: dispatch register --name NAME --role ROLE --description DESC [--capability CAP ...] command
Broker handler creates worker entry with unique UUID, name, role, description, and capability strings
Capability values are plain strings, optionally using name:description convention
Returns worker ID as machine-readable JSON on stdout
Worker has TTL-based liveness with configurable default expiry
Role is stored as-is with no interpretation or permission enforcement
Tests for registration, ID uniqueness, and capability storage
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Team listing and worker heartbeat
userStories.description: As a user, I want to list active workers with dispatch team and renew worker liveness with dispatch heartbeat so long-running tasks do not cause expiry.
acceptanceCriteria: dispatch team outputs active workers as JSON array with id, name, role, description, and capabilities
dispatch heartbeat --worker-id ID renews the worker TTL
Expired workers are excluded from team listing automatically
Heartbeat returns success confirmation as JSON on stdout
Error if worker ID not found or already expired
Tests for team listing, heartbeat renewal, and TTL-based expiry
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Direct message sending
userStories.description: As a worker or caller, I want to send a direct message to a registered worker by ID and have it queued in their mailbox.
acceptanceCriteria: dispatch send --to WORKER_ID --body MESSAGE [--from SENDER_ID] command
Message struct includes sender identity, recipient identity, message body, and unique message ID
Broker queues message in the recipient worker's mailbox
Returns message ID as machine-readable JSON on stdout
Error with clear message if recipient worker ID not found or expired
Tests for message sending, mailbox queuing, and unknown recipient error
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Listen loop with long-poll
userStories.description: As a worker, I want to long-poll my mailbox with dispatch listen and receive the next queued message or a timeout indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: dispatch listen --worker-id ID [--timeout SECS] command
Long-polls the broker and returns next queued message as JSON
Timeout result is a distinct JSON shape from a message result
Listen implicitly renews worker liveness TTL
Default timeout if not specified
Tests for message delivery via listen, timeout behavior, and liveness renewal
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stale and refresh control message examples
userStories.description: As a user, I want documentation and examples showing how to send stale/refresh control messages to workers using dispatch send.
acceptanceCriteria: README or docs section explains stale/refresh as ordinary direct messages with a body convention
Example shows sending a stale message with file path as scope detail
Example shows sending a refresh message with folder path as scope
Example shows a worker reacting to a refresh message in its listen loop
Dispatch does not inspect, parse, or manage worker caches itself
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Filesystem watcher example script
userStories.description: As a user, I want a reference watcher script that observes filesystem changes and sends stale/refresh messages through the Dispatch CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: Shell script in examples/ that watches one or more filesystem paths for changes
Applies a debounce window before sending messages
Demonstrates both file-level and folder-level freshness signaling
Uses the public dispatch send command, not a privileged internal API
Documentation explains how to connect the watcher to a specific worker in the active cell
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: End-to-end multi-role workflow example
userStories.description: As a user, I want a runnable example showing planner, implementer, reviewer, test runner, and shipper workers coordinating a coding workflow in one cell.
acceptanceCriteria: Runnable example or harness in examples/ with 5 workers: planner, code.implementer, code.reviewer, test.runner, code.shipper
Each worker registers itself and waits in a dispatch listen loop for work
Workflow demonstrates plan -> implement -> review -> test -> ship sequence
Worker behavior comes from how the process is launched, not a Dispatch permission model
Runs locally on one machine without any external message broker
Documentation explains the workflow steps and how to run the example
Typecheck passes
